description: Gold: {anchor: wf, embedsOther: false} — seven steps but zero branches or reasoning; length is not autonomy. Complements adv-many-tools-still-wf (tool count) by probing step count. Ported from the intent-resolution spec (§5.1 wf-long-linear-order-fulfillment).

conversation.text: When a new order arrives: validate address, check inventory, charge the card, generate invoice PDF, email the customer, add to CRM, notify fulfillment. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
If the assistant asks a clarifying question, give a minimal plausible answer.
Once it has laid out its approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: The agent proposes a workflow of fixed sequential steps for the order pipeline and does not propose an n8n Agent.
The proposed design contains no orchestrating agent step or AI Agent node — every one of the seven steps is a fixed action.
The proposal does not treat the length of the pipeline as a reason for agency — the justification rests on the fixed, enumerable shape of the steps.
